description: CLI Scan Report & First Impression — wire scan engine to polished CLI with clap, indicatif progress, owo-colors, and formatted report output

userStories.title: Basic seshat scan command with clap and two-phase progress
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `seshat scan <path>` and see scanning progress so that I know Seshat is working and how long it will take.
acceptanceCriteria: clap v4 added to workspace dependencies with derive feature; wired in seshat-bin/src/main.rs with derive-based subcommand parsing
Subcommands defined: scan (implemented), serve/status/review/init (stubbed with 'not yet implemented' message and non-zero exit)
seshat scan <path> validates path exists and is a directory; prints 'error: {message}' + 'hint: {suggestion}' if not
seshat --version / -V prints 'seshat {version} ({git_hash})' using build.rs to capture git short hash
Version header displayed at scan start: 'seshat v{version}'
indicatif added to workspace dependencies; Phase 1: 'Discovering files... {count} found' with in-place count update via indicatif spinner
Phase 2: 'Scanning ████████░░░░ {done}/{total} [{elapsed}]' progress bar with known total
Progress callback mechanism added to scan_project() — either Fn(ScanProgress) parameter or Arc<AtomicUsize> shared counter
Scan pipeline executes end-to-end: discovery → parse → detect → aggregate → store
Database created in XDG data directory (dirs::data_dir()/seshat/repos/{project_name}.db) — dirs crate added to workspace
AppConfig loaded from seshat.toml if present, otherwise defaults
tracing-subscriber initialized with appropriate log level
clap types defined in seshat-cli crate — seshat-bin stays thin (parse args → delegate to cli crate)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Output formatting utilities, owo-colors, verbosity and NO_COLOR support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent CLI formatting with verbosity control and color support so that output is readable and the formatting infrastructure is reusable for all report sections.
acceptanceCriteria: owo-colors added to workspace dependencies for colored output
Shared formatting functions extracted to seshat-cli/src/format.rs: format_section_header(), format_bar_chart(), format_tier_bullet(), format_human_size(), format_error_hint(), format_bordered_box()
Section headers use box-drawing format: '── {Title} ──────...' padded to ~60 chars
All messages follow '{level}: {message}' pattern: error:, warn:, info:, hint:
Error output: 'error: {message}' (red) + blank line + 'hint: {suggestion}' lines
NO_COLOR environment variable respected — disables all color output; color application centralized in one place
--quiet flag: errors + final summary line only — no warnings, no findings list
Default (no flags): errors + warnings + summary + key findings
--verbose flag adds: skipped files section with reasons, detector details table, timing breakdown
Code/config snippets bordered box utility with '┌─┐│└─┘' characters implemented for future init command
Number formatting utilities: thousands separator, human-readable file sizes
Unit tests for format_section_header(), format_bar_chart(), format_tier_bullet(), format_human_size()
Unit tests for verbosity filtering: quiet/default/verbose produce correct output
Unit test for NO_COLOR handling
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must come before US-003 and US-004 since they depend on shared formatting functions from format.rs

userStories.title: Scan report — Project Overview section
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the scan report to show a project overview with language breakdown, module count, and dependency count so that I immediately see what Seshat learned about my project.
acceptanceCriteria: Section header: '── Project Overview ──────────...' using format_section_header() from format.rs
Language breakdown with horizontal bar charts using ▓ (filled) and ░ (empty), percentage, and file count — sorted by percentage descending
Module count and dependency count with ecosystem breakdown in parentheses (e.g., '127 packages (98 npm, 29 pip)')
Submodules section displayed if applicable: '{path}/ → {language} project ({count} files)'
Data sourced from ScanResult + database queries (language counts from files_ir, dependencies from declared_dependencies, modules from nodes where type=module)
Report rendering code lives in seshat-cli/src/report/ — not mixed into scan logic
seshat-cli does NOT depend on seshat-storage directly — gets report data via seshat-graph queries or from scan result
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (scan command) and US-002 (formatting utilities). 'Verify in browser' here means run seshat scan on a test project and verify terminal output.

userStories.title: Scan report — Conventions Detected and Next Steps sections
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the scan report to show detected conventions with confidence tiers and trend indicators, plus copy-paste next steps, so that I see immediate value from Seshat.
acceptanceCriteria: Section header: '── Conventions Detected ({count}) ──────...' using format_section_header()
Confidence tier summary with Unicode bullets: ● high (>85%), ◐ medium (50-85%), ○ low (<50%) using format_tier_bullet()
Top findings list: tier bullet + description + trend arrow + confidence percentage, sorted by confidence descending
Trend indicators: ↑ Rising, ─ Stable, ↓ Declining, (space) Unknown
Default shows top 10 conventions; --verbose shows all
Next Steps section with copy-paste commands: 'Run seshat review to validate detected conventions', 'Run seshat serve to start MCP server', 'Run seshat init to generate MCP config'
Summary line: '{count} conventions detected. Run seshat review to validate.'
Database path with human-readable size: 'Database: ~/.local/share/seshat/repos/my-project.db (12.4 MB)' using format_human_size()
Conventions queried from knowledge graph (KnowledgeNode where nature=Convention/Observation) with confidence and trend from ext_data
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (formatting utilities) and US-003 (overview section already wired into report). 'Verify in browser' here means run seshat scan and verify terminal output.

userStories.title: Branch code review and quality gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a comprehensive code review of all changes in the feature branch before merging so that the code meets professional Rust quality standards.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo fmt --all --check passes with no formatting violations
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes with no lint warnings
cargo test --workspace passes with all tests passing
cargo doc --no-deps --document-private-items builds without warnings
No new unsafe blocks without justification
No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code (only in tests and main.rs where appropriate)
Error handling uses thiserror for error types, ? for propagation
CLI argument types use Rust type system: PathBuf for paths, enums for subcommands, Option for optional flags
Doc comments (///) on all public items; module-level //! on each new module
No unnecessary allocations in report rendering — format strings efficiently, bar chart uses fixed-size buffer
Progress bar updates throttled to avoid terminal flicker
Database queries for report data batched — no N+1 queries
ScanResult and report data properly scoped — dropped after display
Shared formatting extracted to seshat-cli/src/format.rs — no copy-pasted formatting logic between overview, conventions, and verbose sections
clap types in seshat-cli — not in seshat-bin; report rendering in seshat-cli/src/report/ — not mixed into scan logic
Unit tests for formatting functions and verbosity filtering exist
Integration test: run seshat scan on a temp project directory, verify exit code and key output patterns
Test error cases: nonexistent path, empty directory, directory with no parseable files
Full git diff main...HEAD reviewed for code duplication, architecture compliance, and idiomatic Rust
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final quality gate — review all changes from US-001 through US-004. Run all automated checks and perform manual diff review.
